description_long: This kiosk is modeled after a traditional Chinese paifang or gateway, featuring three arched passageways supported by four robust columns. The roof is tiered and ornate, with upturned eaves and decorative ridges, evoking classic East Asian architectural motifs. The central arch is slightly larger than the two flanking arches, emphasizing its role as the main entryway. The structure is rendered in a uniform, matte white material, giving it a stone or plaster-like appearance. Subtle engravings or reliefs are present above the central arch, suggesting space for signage or inscriptions. The overall form is symmetrical and monumental, designed to serve as a prominent landmark or ceremonial entrance in an urban setting.
description: A white, ornate kiosk resembling a traditional Chinese gateway with three arches and a tiered, upturned roof. The structure is symmetrical, with decorative details and a monumental presence.
description_view_0: Front view showing three arched passageways, four columns, and detailed roof eaves.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure and the layered, upturned roof profile.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with three arches and similar decorative roof features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the depth and tiered roof design.
attribute_signage_type: engraved sign above the central archway, no visible text
attribute_door_configuration: three open archways at the front and rear
attribute_roof_design: tiered, upturned eaves with decorative ridges, traditional East Asian style, solid material
